title: Legal Firm Relocations: Strict Chain-of-Custody Packing Methods for Active Court Case Archives
excerpt: Legal firm relocation: court case archives chain of custody, active litigation files, secure packing, law office move Addis Ababa confidentiality protocols.
content:
Legal firm relocations: strict chain-of-custody packing methods for active court case archives

Law firm moves are custody events—active court case archives cannot mingle with closed files or vanish in unlabeled carton. Bar rules, client confidentiality, and litigation deadlines demand chain-of-custody packing: numbered boxes, sign-off logs, and transport that survives audit if opposing counsel questions integrity.

This guide covers chain-of-custody methods for legal firm relocations with active case archives.

File classification before pack

  • Active litigation: Separate color; daily access plan at destination.
  • Closed matters: Archive tier; offsite storage option reduces move volume.
  • Privileged communications: Encrypted transport list; no open truck overnight unsecured.
  • Original exhibits: Double-box; photo condition; sometimes court permission move.

Chain-of-custody documentation

Box ID sequential; matter number range on exterior index; log signer at seal, load, unload, shelve. Tamper-evident seals on active matter cartons. Partner or records officer countersigns high-stakes boxes.

Packing standards

Legal file boxes upright; no spine crush; climate avoid damp truck. USB and evidence media separate custody log—serialized. Library treatises heavy—distribute weight.

Transport security

Enclosed truck; named crew NDAs; no subcontract surprise. After-hours move reduces foot traffic witness. GPS track if firm policy requires.

Destination shelving order

Active matter shelves live before decorative—clerks find file Monday hearing. Docket calendar cross-check nothing missing pre-court.

IT and e-discovery parallel

Server and backup media IT vendor—chain mirrors paper. Email archive DNS cutover documented.

Active Movers legal sector tier

Custody log templates; vetted crew list; enclosed transport standard—disclose active matter box count at RFP.

Conclusion

Legal firm archive moves require classified packing, tamper seals, signed custody chain, and secure enclosed transport—active cases survive relocation when integrity documented box by box.
author: Active Movers Team
